item.title: Too cold to exercise? Here's how to start and stay on track
item.description: Experts say winter is a good time to double down on exercise rather than taking the foot off the gas.

item.title: No subscriptions? No problems! Here are the best movies and TV series you can watch free
item.description: From Bluey in Yolŋu Matha to the first feature film directed by an Indigenous woman, there is a trove of free movies and TV to dive into this NAIDOC Week.

item.title: Use your phone for work? This is how you can claim it as a tax deduction
item.description: The cost of buying and using your own phone may be tax deductible, but the ATO has specific rules about how employees calculate these expenses.

item.title: Donald Trump teaches crypto devotees a valuable lesson
item.description: US President Donald Trump should have been a godsend to the mysterious world of cryptocurrency. Instead, it has been a godsend to him.

item.title: Activists urge Australia to sign treaty to ban nuclear weapons
item.description: There are renewed calls for Australia to sign a treaty ensuring nuclear weapons can't ever be facilitated, stored or manufactured in Australia.

item.title: WorkSafe investigating after Melbourne footballer Nathan Fitzgerald suffers life-ending head injuries
item.description: Victoria's workplace safety watchdog is investigating after a suburban footy player was injured, and later died, after his head collided with a cricket pitch.

item.title: Germany cracks down on employees calling in sick by phone
item.description: Chucking a sickie is no longer as easy as a quick phone call in Germany, under new laws hoped to boost productivity. Here's how the world is responding.

item.title: When is State of Origin III? Kick-off time and how to watch Maroons vs Blues
item.description: The men's State of Origin series has gone to a decider, with Queensland and New South Wales sharing the spoils after the opening two matches. Here is what you need to know before kick-off of Game III tomorrow night.

item.title: Man behind a bold Launceston CBD development plan may have been running insolvent companies
item.description: The Tasmanian government and a local council welcomed a bold plan to transform Launceston’s CBD from a man who may have been running insolvent companies at the time, an investigation shows.

item.title: Farmer awaits cooler temperatures this week after 'uncharacteristically' mild July
item.description: As Queenslanders brace for cooler temperatures this week, farmers on the Granite Belt are welcoming single digits to ensure their crops are thriving come harvest time.

item.title: World Cup: 'Shameful', incomprehensible': Severe blowback over FIFA's opaque Balogun decision
item.description: FIFA has always insisted it is politically neutral. But its integrity is now being questioned after US President Donald Trump confirmed he asked for Folarin Balogun's suspension to be reviewed.

item.title: 'Bad blood' between US and European allies as NATO summit begins
item.description: Dozens of world leaders are descending on Türkiye this week to rubber-stamp significant NATO commitments, but the dynamics on the sidelines could prove more interesting to watch.

item.title: Three of 'Croatian Six' convicted over alleged terror plot may be innocent, inquiry finds
item.description: An inquiry has raised "reasonable doubt" over the guilt of three of six Croatian men jailed in 1981 over an alleged terrorist plot to bomb several Sydney buildings in 1979.

item.title: US prosecutors brief families on long-delayed trial for accused Bali bombing terror leader
item.description: US military prosecutors reveal new details about their case against the man accused of planning the 2002 Bali bombings, telling the loved ones of Australian victims his trial could finally begin next year.

item.title: Police charge alleged Pam the Bird graffiti vandal after Bolte Bridge stand-off
item.description: A man has faced court after spending hours at the top of a 140-metre-high tower on Melbourne's Bolte Bridge, where he allegedly spray-painted the notorious graffiti symbol.

item.title: SA Museum's 'walking archive' helps First Nations people discover family history
item.description: For 28 years, Ali Abdullah-Highfold has helped thousands of Indigenous people find familial connections through his work at the South Australian Museum.

item.title: Queensland mental health services at risk from 'huge' funding shortfall
item.description: Queensland mental health community services receive funding from government departments based on an indexation rate.

item.title: Alex de Minaur loses in Wimbledon fourth round to Flavio Cobolli
item.description: Alex de Minaur loses in the fourth round at Wimbledon in another match he feels he could, and maybe should, have won against ninth seed Flavio Cobolli.

item.title: Man with prior domestic violence convictions granted a NSW Working with Children Check
item.description: The man's case is one of the last appeals filed in the NSW Civil and Administrative Tribunal before the law changed in September last year.

item.title: Alcoa's looming WA alumina monopoly could threaten world's only jarrah forests, environmentalists say
item.description: Alcoa's $8 billion purchase of South32 could be key to unlocking untouched areas of WA's northern jarrah forest for bauxite mining, raising the concern of environmentalists.

item.title: Alexandra Eala makes another stride for Philippines as Jasmine Paolini ends her Wimbledon run
item.description: For a nation like the Philippines, where sports stars are held on an even higher pedestal, Alexandra Eala is taking her place among the greats.

item.title: Kaniva finally connects to drinkable mains water, but not all residents want it on tap
item.description: Victoria's largest town without drinking water finally gets a new potable supply, but it has left the community that has relied on tank water divided.

item.title: Pacific leaders criticise Chinese nuclear-capable missile test as 'reckless'
item.description: Solomon Islands PM says a ballistic missile test carried out by China is "not the act of a friend", sentiments echoed by several Pacific leaders.

item.title: Paul Papalia's difficult departure leaves WA Labor nervous about One Nation's surge
item.description: Paul Papalia's resignation for family reasons sparks a by-election in the WA minister and Labor stalwart's southern Perth seat at a time when disgruntled voters are increasingly turning to One Nation.

item.title: Ex-champion boxer charged over four kidnappings in Sydney
item.description: Robbery and Serious Crime Squad detectives have charged 26-year-old Humzah Khalid, previously known by his boxing name The Raging Bull, for his alleged involvement in four kidnappings over 10 months.

item.title: Retired judge Christopher Beale reflects on Erin Patterson murder trial in podcast interview
item.description: Retired judge Christopher Beale has reflected on his long legal career, his temporary pivot to becoming a Jesuit priest and the role he played in the Erin Patterson murder trial.

item.title: Family of Gavin Feltwell call for stronger sentencing for mining corporations
item.description: They waited four years for a court outcome, only to be left feeling like it was an injustice.

item.title: Democrats' Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner accused of sexual assault
item.description: Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner says the allegation is "categorically false", but is considering his next steps as the report prompts several key backers to withdraw their endorsements.

item.title: Baby lamb twin boom brings hope to sheep farmer after years of dry
item.description: A wet start to 2026 in regional South Australia has helped create perfect conditions for multiple lamb births on one merino sheep stud.

item.title: Bridget Archer as a stand in for Rockliff is a soft-launch succession plan for Tasmania's Liberals
item.description: Make no mistake, news that maverick Liberal Bridget Archer will be acting Tasmanian premier for a week is a significant step towards her eventually taking over from Jeremy Rockliff — a move sure to enrage those who believe they have a more rightful claim.

item.title: New steel plant for Newcastle nearly three decades on from BHP's closure
item.description: Once dubbed Australia's "steel city", steel production wound down in Newcastle in 1999 when BHP closed its plant. Now, an Australian company says it will build a new steel plant, powered only by electricity.

item.title: Former NRL player Jai Arrow marries partner months after motor neurone disease diagnosis
item.description: The former Rabbitohs and Queensland NRL player has married his partner Berina Colakovic in a small ceremony in Sydney, months after revealing his motor neurone disease diagnosis.

item.title: Cherbourg council and elders address community after teenagers vandalise cemetery
item.description: The Cherbourg Aboriginal Shire Council releases a video to the community saying "enough is enough" after local teenagers are charged for vandalising the cemetery.

item.title: Man denied bail after drugs allegedly seized from vehicle in prison car park
item.description: Wayne Douglas Carnell has been remanded in custody after police allegedly found 100 grams of methamphetamine in his vehicle which was searched in a prison car park.

item.title: ADFA cadet facing 79 additional charges after allegedly using hidden bathroom camera to film women
item.description: Police allege Tain Stephens was living at the Australian Defence Force Academy in Canberra when he hid a camera inside a soap dispenser in a female bathroom to capture intimate recordings of women.

item.title: AFL legend Tony Modra leaves hospital after truck crash near Victor Harbor
item.description: Former Adelaide Crows and Fremantle player Tony Modra says he has left hospital after he was seriously injured in a truck crash last month.

item.title: Why State of Origin legacies and futures are on the line for New South Wales in Game III
item.description: As New South Wales look to rise from the ashes and pull off a Lang Park Origin heist, the future of the team and the legacy of some of its greatest players will be tested in the furnace of a decider.

item.title: NRL announces new broadcast rights deal worth estimated $5.3 billion starting after 2027 season
item.description: As the NRL reveals its new broadcast rights deal worth billions of dollars over seven years in 2028 through 2034, executives try to hose down concerns about rising subscription prices.

item.title: With one missile, China sends world a reminder of its growing military power
item.description: This latest military test demonstrates China's growing willingness to use its military strength to send a warning to the Pacific: that Beijing now has the military and nuclear means to assert its dominance in the region.

item.title: Government commits to restore current Paradise Dam wall over new build
item.description: The Queensland government announces a plan to rebuild the Paradise Dam wall where it is, despite its operator previously saying there were too many issues to fix it.

item.title: A frame-by-frame look at the tackle that earned Folarin Balogun his controversial red card
item.description: While many agree the red card call was too harsh, the story has become a bigger discussion about FIFA's integrity.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: American dream comes crashing down, Kylian Mbappé hits back at abuse
item.description: The US goes crashing out of its own World Cup after days of controversy and rancour, while Kylian Mbappé refuses to stand for dreadful racist abuse. Here are today's quick hits.

item.title: Fishing ban lifted early in SA amid positive signs for King George whiting
item.description: A temporary ban on recreational catches of King George whiting along parts of the SA coast is lifted earlier than scheduled, in a move that fishers say will benefit the whole sector.

item.title: Widow fights for answers over death of Laurence Harbeck on INPEX LNG plant
item.description: A Perth widow says she has been kept in the dark about the circumstances leading to the death of her husband as he worked on an offshore gas plant.

item.title: World Cup: Belgium consoles Folarin Balogun but claims 'justice' was served in 4-1 win over US
item.description: Belgium did not feel it was fair Folarin Balogun played for the US in the round of 16 match after he was given a red card in his team's previous match.

item.title: Accused double killer Beau Lamarre-Condon's mother Coleen Lamarre granted bail
item.description: Coleen Lamarre has been in custody since April, when she was arrested in Sydney's inner west accused of attempting to influence a key witness in her son's upcoming double murder trial.

item.title: Launceston General Hospital emergency department overflowing, nurses union says
item.description: Launceston General Hospital's emergency department was overflowing on Monday night, with the nursing union describing the scenes as "chaotic" and "unsafe" — as Tasmania's health minister blames the federal government for a lack of action.

item.title: Suspected bird flu identified in giant petrel on SA coast, but testing to determine if H5
item.description: A suspected H5 bird flu case has been detected in a giant petrel on SA's Yorke Peninsula, but the state government says the viral load is low, with further results expected within the next 48 hours.

item.title: Football club will hold service to honour Nathan Fitzgerald following fatal game day injury
item.description: The younger brother of footy player and teacher Nathan Fitzgerald, who died last night, has described him as a "gentle, wonderful" soul who cherished his family.

item.title: Report suggesting DPP Sally Dowling be removed over media leak condemned by attorney-general
item.description: The inquiry into children's identity protection was called in October last year, but has focused almost solely on the leaking of the story to media.

item.title: Man held by security staff during alleged bottle shop assault and robbery at Northgate, north of Hobart
item.description: Security staff restrain a suspect during an alleged assault and robbery at a bottle shop in Hobart's north.

item.title: Piggery owners face court on animal cruelty charges
item.description: Five co-owners of an Adelaide Plains piggery are collectively accused of ill-treating their animals, including allegedly failing to ensure the animals had "adequate and appropriate living conditions".

item.title: Tuesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Toddler found in swimming pool and declared dead later found alive at hospital
item.description: Five hours after the young boy was pronounced dead next to a suburban swimming pool in the US state of Arizona, police were notified that the child was breathing again at a local hospital.

item.title: Is FIFA’s integrity at stake after Donald Trump’s interference?
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Ukrainian prosecutors confirm Monaco bomb suspect found dead
item.description: A Ukrainian woman suspected of a bomb attack that targeted a wealthy Ukrainian-born businessman in Monaco has been found shot dead in Ukraine, according to Ukraine's prosecutor general's office.

item.title: Prince Harry, Elton John and others lose privacy lawsuits against the Daily Mail
item.description: Prince Harry and six other claimants, including singer Elton John, have lost their claims against Daily Mail's publisher after alleging their phones were tapped, voicemails intercepted and personal information obtained through deception.

item.title: Messi leads incredible Argentina comeback before Swiss shootout delight
item.description: Lionel Messi guides Argentina to an incredible comeback win against Egypt, coming back from 2-0 down to steal a 3-2 win at the death, while Switzerland knocks Colombia out in a penalty shootout.

item.title: Travel quiz, July 8: Jump in, we're going on a trip around the world
item.description: Which Mediterranean island was a setting for The Godfather? And what's the capital city of Morocco?

item.title: Deadly podcasts to listen to this NAIDOC Week, from Indigenuity to Inside the Huddle
item.description: Some of our favourite Indigenous-led podcasts this week and every week include interviews with AFL greats, a dive into Aboriginal astronomy, and conversations with a renowned artist who loves Dolly Parton.

item.title: PBS dispute could leave MS patients with huge bills for crucial drugs Ocrevus and Kesimpta
item.description: The latest PBS stoush with drugmakers means thousands of people with MS are at risk of being forced to pay $33,000 a year for lifesaving medication.

item.title: How this school cracked the code to close the gap in early reading
item.description: An inspiring turnaround at an Aboriginal community school could provide a scalable solution for closing the gap on Indigenous literacy.

item.title: What time is State of Origin III kick-off? How to watch the Maroons vs Blues decider
item.description: The men's State of Origin series will be decided tonight, as Queensland hosts New South Wales at Lang Park in a winner-takes-all Game III. Here is what you need to know ahead of kick-off.

item.title: Teammates of Melbourne footballer Nathan Fitzgerald to sit out match after death
item.description: Epping football club's reserves players have decided to skip this weekend's game following the death of their beloved teammate Nathan Fitzgerald on Monday night.

item.title: Country footy player finds new sport after suffering eight concussions
item.description: Angus Pitman finds a new sporting passion after walking away from country footy due to coping eight concussions on the field.

item.title: Queensland government on track to make almost $5 billion a year from land tax as revenue grows
item.description: Over the next four years the government's annual revenue from the tax is forecast to climb by almost 80 per cent to $4.8 billion.

item.title: China missile launch tests Pacific relations as Australia looks to pounce
item.description: This week, perhaps for the first time in a long time, some sections of the Pacific are signalling a slight adjustment to their relationship with China.

item.title: Oral care giant Hismile used 'misleading' videos in social media advertisements
item.description: Oral care giant Hismile has used "misleading" videos in social media advertisements showing a sales professional, and its own staff, spruiking products while dressed as health workers.

item.title: Outback becomes oasis of birds, wildlife after 'unbelievable' desert rains
item.description: Winged explorers of all colours, shapes and sizes flock to the desert's edge to breed in the bustling wetlands of the Channel Country.

item.title: Algae which caused brevetoxins in SA algal bloom most toxic species studied of its kind
item.description: The dominant species in the algal bloom which devastated SA's coastline, Karenia cristata, is the most toxic of its kind studied, according to newly published research.

item.title: First-of-its-kind government report says AI not yet causing mass job lay-offs
item.description: Artificial intelligence is not yet causing a broad disruption to Australia's labour market, according to a government report.

item.title: CFA senior leaders accidentally send 'draft' response to fatal bushfire inquiry
item.description: An administrative error during a parliamentary inquiry has led the CFA to inadvertently reveal exactly how short-staffed its stations were during last summer's deadly fire season.

item.title: Telstra outage: 'Largely returned to business as usual' after nationwide crash
item.description: Communications Minister Anika Wells says Telstra has "largely returned to business as usual" after its network suffered a nationwide outage that impacted potentially millions of Australians.

item.title: Seventeen dead, hundreds injured as China braces for more wild weather
item.description: China's president calls for "all out" rescue efforts after storms kill at least 17 people and force the evacuation of tens of thousands.

item.title: Iran targets sites in Bahrain and Kuwait after wave of US strikes
item.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards target US military sites in Bahrain and Kuwait after the US launched a wave of military strikes on Iran.

item.title: Naomi Osaka says she's getting old after Wimbledon quarterfinal loss to Karolina Muchova
item.description: Japan's Naomi Osaka bemoans getting old as her inspiring Wimbledon run comes to an end in the quarterfinals.

item.title: World Cup: Egypt coach Hossam Hassan fumes at ref after Argentina loss
item.description: Egypt coach Hossam Hassan says his side "suffered an injustice" in defeat to Argentina, raging at the referee for a number of perceived errors.

item.title: Housing inequity in Australia 'must focus beyond age'
item.description: The federal government must look at age and wealth together to meaningfully deal with the growing wealth divide between generations, according to The Australian Council of Social Services (ACOSS).

item.title: Telstra outage brings interstate trains, Victorian regional rail network to a halt
item.description: A crippling statewide cancellation of Victoria's regional trains, linked to a Telstra outage, is set to continue disrupting services into Thursday morning.

item.title: Great Barrier Reef's long-term monitoring program celebrates 40 years
item.description: One of the researchers, who is often towed underwater to count fish and coral on the Great Barrier Reef, says it feels like flying.

item.title: Henderson 'not going to apologise' for test calls to Triple Zero, Telstra CFO says service now restored — as it happened
item.description: Telstra says services have been restored after its mobile network suffered a nationwide outage on Wednesday morning.

item.title: New York high-rise at risk of 'collapse' after interior columns buckle, sparking evacuations
item.description: The former headquarters of pharmaceutical giant Pfizer was being converted into luxury apartments when buckled interior columns were discovered inside the building.

item.title: Lancelin residents try to self-fund sea wall to stop imminent erosion
item.description: Lancelin residents have been left to fundraise $150,000 for a sea wall in a bid to fight coastal erosion now seriously threatening the town, more than 100 kilometres north of Perth.

item.title: Novak Djokovic wins five-hour Wimbledon epic over Felix Auger-Aliassime to set up Jannik Sinner semi
item.description: Tennis legend Novak Djokovic sets up a Wimbledon semifinal blockbuster against Jannik Sinner after a five-hour epic.

item.title: European politicians launch bid for investigation into FIFA boss Gianni Infantino red card controversy
item.description: A group of European politicians launches a bid to begin an investigation into how and why a red card for an American football star was overturned at the World Cup.

item.title: Toddler waits seven hours at Bendigo Health amid pressure on emergency department
item.description: A Central Victorian mother says she waited seven hours at Bendigo Health for her toddler to be treated for a head wound.

item.title: Shopping seasonally this July looks like ...
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Australia's doubles fly the flag at Wimbledon as Marc Polmans and Storm Hunter make mixed final
item.description: Marc Polmans and Storm Hunter win through to the Wimbledon mixed doubles final, as Thanasi Kokkinakis flies the flag in the men's doubles.

item.title: Inside Guts: the solar-powered music tour connecting remote NT communities and Australian bands
item.description: Behind the scenes of the Guts tour: a roaming, solar-powered tour that snakes its way through some of the most remote communities in the Northern Territory, bringing together bands from the city and the bush to hold concerts in areas that don't host a lot of touring musicians.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Egypt coach blows up over 'fixed' tournament as Belgium trolls Trump
item.description: An all-time blow-up from Egypt's coach headlines the day at the World Cup, as the fallout from Belgium's win over the US continues. Here are today's quick hits.

item.title: Four fined after allegedly illegally fishing in green zone during Queensland competition
item.description: The group was caught allegedly fishing illegally in a no-take zone during a competition. Four people have been fined almost $15,000.

item.title: World Cup: Despite gut-wrenching loss, Egyptian diaspora celebrates historic campaign
item.description: After initial disappointment after a late loss to Argentina, many Egypt football fans are joyously reflecting on the team's historic campaign, while using the stage to show support for Palestinians.

item.title: CLP politician Jo Hersey claiming travel allowance when staying at property she owns in Darwin CBD
item.description: It comes after Jo Hersey's CLP colleague criticised Labor politician Manuel Brown for claiming the same allowance at times when staying at his Palmerston home.

item.title: Passenger, freight and coal trains halted in parts of NSW amid Telstra outage
item.description: Services in the Hunter, between Sydney and Canberra, and around the Southern Highlands have been suspended. Meanwhile, some coal and freight trains have been halted for safety, amid the outage.

item.title: Business fined $1,200 after sparking fire ant scare in Central Queensland
item.description: The unnamed business shipped a pallet of second-hand bricks infested with fire ants to mine sites in the Bowen Basin in mid-2025, sparking four rounds of aerial baiting at a cost of $1 million.

item.title: US Central Command releases vision of Iran strikes
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: BHP staff confirm historic strike action at mining giant's Port Hedland operations
item.description: Unionised workers at the mining giant's operations in Port Hedland will down tools for eight hours on July 16.

item.title: Hikers warned to take Adelaide's peri-urban parks seriously after rescue
item.description: Three teenagers were rescued from Onkaparinga River National Park unscathed on Sunday, showing how quickly hikers can come unstuck on spectacular walking trails despite their proximity to Adelaide.

item.title: Sale of Tasmania's biggest farm, Rushy Lagoon, to UK pine plantation investor approved
item.description: The sale of Tasmania's biggest farm to the UK's largest forestry investor has been given the green light — with the news greeted by outrage from the state's farmers' group.

item.title: Former NSW Liberal minister John Sidoti charged with misconduct over property redevelopment pressure
item.description: Former New South Wales Liberal government minister John Sidoti says he will fight a charge of misconduct while in public office.

item.title: Time-keeping technology triggers Telstra nationwide outage. Here's what we know
item.description: Telstra says Wednesday's nationwide outage was caused by malfunctioning "nodes" that manage time synchronisation within some of the network data centres, but has no idea what caused the timer issues.

item.title: The devastation of ACL injuries can get lost in how prolific they are, says Essendon's Emily Gough
item.description: Essendon AFLW forward Emily Gough said devastating ACL injuries can get lost in the sheer amount of them in women's game.

item.title: Seven arrested as police probe alleged fraud involving Greater Shepparton City Council
item.description: The men were arrested by detectives as part of an ongoing investigation after a council in Victoria's north says it referred alleged fraud involving council employees to police.

item.title: Melbourne businessman pleads not guilty to raping woman
item.description: The businessman, who cannot be identified for legal reasons, is accused of attacking a woman at his home in 2023.

item.title: Brothers accused of murdering stepfather outside Melbourne primary school face court
item.description: DNA testing in the investigation of two brothers accused of murdering their stepfather outside a Melbourne primary school could take up to three months, a court hears.

item.title: Australian woman arrested in US after allegedly voting illegally in federal elections
item.description: An Australian woman has been arrested in the United States after an ICE investigation allegedly found she had illegally voted in multiple federal elections.

item.title: World Cup quarterfinals: When and where as France, England and Argentina take next step
item.description: The World Cup has whittled 48 teams down to just eight, with the quarterfinals upon us and the finish line in sight. Find out all you need to know about these monumental matches.

item.title: Investigation into former NACC commissioner Paul Brereton discontinued over cost
item.description: Investigations into allegations against Paul Brereton will end after the anti-corruption inspector found they would be too expensive to pursue.

item.title: Lithium-ion vape battery sets man's pants on fire in Byron Bay
item.description: A Brisbane man visiting Byron Bay has been left with burns after a lithium-ion battery ignited in his pocket.

item.title: Proposed building code throws Queensland's construction industry into confusion, unions say
item.description: Queensland Council of Unions general secretary Jacqueline King has called on Deputy Premier Jarrod Bleijie to explain the proposed changes to the industry.

item.title: China-linked investors' refusal to divest from WA heavy rare earths company 'extraordinary', expert says
item.description: China-linked investors in a Western Australia rare earths company have yet to comply with federal Treasurer Jim Chalmers's latest order to sell their shares.

item.title: Court case against chair of Indian steelmaker won't affect Whyalla sale, SA government says
item.description: The chairperson of one of the bidders for the stricken Whyalla steelworks has been summonsed to appear before an Indian court, but the government says its confident the sale process will maintain integrity.

item.title: Alleged Mongols associate Isaac Thompson arrested, more than 48 hours after escape from custody
item.description: Northern Territory police officers have arrested Isaac Thompson, 26, in Darwin River, more than 48 hours after he escaped custody during a medical transfer.

item.title: Second case of H5 bird flu confirmed in SA, as authorities test two further suspected cases
item.description: South Australia confirms its second H5 bird flu case while Western Australia investigates two more suspected detections, as authorities say the virus has not spread beyond isolated wild birds.

item.title: Crown prosecutors issue joint statement in support of Sally Dowling after controversial DPP report
item.description: Senior crown prosecutor Brett Hatfield SC issued the extraordinary statement on behalf of the more than 120 barristers who take on cases for the crown.

item.title: Closing arguments begin in trial of former Wagga Wagga MP Daryl Maguire
item.description: A court hears former Wagga Wagga MP Daryl Maguire knew being dishonest to the Department of Immigration was a component of an alleged fraudulent visa scheme.

item.title: Cleary 'silences the critics' as NSW make Origin history with decider win
item.description: NSW has produced a historic performance to win the State of Origin series with a 30-12 victory over Queensland at Lang Park in Brisbane.

item.title: How the Telstra outage impacted these Australians
item.description: Millions have potentially been impacted by the Telstra outage. Here's how it affected Australians doing basic everyday things, from making calls to measuring blood sugar levels.

item.title: Byssal mussel project at Cockburn Sound aims to boost ecosystem health
item.description: A restorative mussel farm could improve water quality and biodiversity in Cockburn Sound and the project leaders say there's no need to stop there.

item.title: Trump says US–Iran ceasefire over, warning 'we're going to hit them hard'
item.description: Donald Trump says the ceasefire with Iran is over, warning that the US would likely engage in additional strikes, following fresh attacks across the Gulf.

item.title: Timeline shows delay before Telstra alerted minister to network outage
item.description: Telstra has defended its communication process, insisting the government and other stakeholders were informed "very, very early" in the morning.

item.title: Wednesday Finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Former UOW governance officer Alyssa White takes stand at ICAC inquiry
item.description: Former University of Wollongong chief governance officer Alyssa White takes the witness box at the ICAC inquiry after weeks of evidence examining her role in recruitment decisions, governance reforms and a proposed executive position.

item.title: This Place: Djimubarn, sacred rock art shelter
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Australia issued blunt warning over AUKUS and China's nuclear capabilities
item.description: Victor Gao, the vice-president of a major Chinese think tank, conducts some sabre-rattling in the wake of China's missile test in the Pacific.

item.title: ACT paramedics experience occupational violence every two days
item.description: ACT Ambulance Service recorded more than 190 occupational violence incidents last financial year.

item.title: Trump says US-Iran ceasefire is 'over'
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: State of Origin quick hits: Jai Arrow presents shield as Queensland dudded by bunker
item.description: Jai Arrow is the only Queenslander to touch the State of Origin trophy as the Blues dominate the decider, but a NSW great questions a pivotal try. Here are your quick hits from the State of Origin decider.

item.title: New South Wales keep the faith to write a new destiny in blood after epic State of Origin decider
item.description: Against all odds and with most of their state doubting them, Laurie Daley's New South Wales side has pulled off one of the greatest wins in Blues history.

item.title: Australians face record-high rents across all capital cities: Domain report
item.description: Rents are soaring in several capital cities as low vacancy rates collide with rising interest rates and population growth.

item.title: NRL admits bunker missed knock-on in crucial NSW State of Origin try
item.description: The NRL admits the bunker missed a NSW knock-on in the lead-up to a crucial try that helped the Blues win Wednesday night's State of Origin decider at Lang Park.

item.title: Ruth Ellis receives pardon from king 70 years after hanging in UK
item.description: The case of Ruth Ellis has gripped Britain for decades and inspired the 1985 film Dance with a Stranger.

item.title: Donald Trump says Iran is 'scum' as Ukraine gets big Patriot missile win at wild NATO summit
item.description: Donald Trump says his country will probably continue to attack Iran and says Ukraine will be given permission to manufacture much-needed Patriot missiles as it fights Russia's full-scale invasion.

item.title: 2026 Emmy nominations revealed, with The Pitt and Hacks topping list
item.description: The Pitt takes the lead as the most nominated series for the 78th annual Primetime Emmy Awards, with Hacks in second place.

item.title: Live entertainment industry calls for government lifeline as more musicals close
item.description: As two more major musicals bite the dust, the local theatre industry hopes a live performance incentive modelled on a successful UK scheme will provide salvation.

item.title: Increased demand and new varieties set Australian coffee industry up for expansion
item.description: Australians are among the world's biggest coffee drinkers but less than 1 per cent of the coffee consumed nationwide is grown locally. That could be about to change.

item.title: TV quote quiz: Guess the shows, then put them in order
item.description: Now for the story of 20 famous TV quotes, and the one quiz that had no choice but to keep them all together.

item.title: US carries out strikes on Iran after Donald Trump declares ceasefire 'over'
item.description: The United States military is carrying out renewed strikes on Iran aimed at further degrading its ability to "threaten freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz", after US President Donald Trump earlier threatened a "big attack" was coming.

item.title: Questions asked about telco regulation after Telstra suffers outage
item.description: Advocates have demanded changes to how telecommunication companies are regulated and held accountable for outages after Telstra became the latest telco to suffer a large-scale network failure.

item.title: How snow machines have become the key ingredient for ski seasons
item.description: Data shows snow depth has been declining in Australia since the 1950s, with resorts turning to snowmaking to guarantee a strong ski season.

item.title: Report shows ambulance call-outs plummet during initial years of Richmond's medically supervised injecting room
item.description: Data from a new paper shows the number of heroin-related ambulance call-outs dropped from 48 a month to 14 a month in the first five years following the Richmond medically supervised injecting room's opening.

item.title: Andy Burnham is styled as the everyman, but critics say Britain's next PM is a ruthless 'career politician'
item.description: For some, Andy Burnham's casual communication style is refreshing. Others say it's a carefully crafted facade, disguising the fact that he's a ruthless Labour Party lifer.

item.title: Company behind rejected Hobart 'Stadium 2.0' concept in liquidation
item.description: Tasmanian developer Dean Coleman's company, which was involved in the rejected 'Stadium 2.0' plan for Hobart, is in liquidation. He's now involved in a separate development proposal at Regatta Point.

item.title: Queensland has the most motorcycle deaths in Australia for five years, and the numbers are rising in 2026
item.description: Police say they've seen a rise in anti-social hooning behaviour as Queensland records a rise in fatal motorcycle crashes.

item.title: Aboriginal activist Ruth Langford tells Tasmanian court of obligation to prevent 'slaughtered' forests
item.description: An Aboriginal activist has told a Tasmanian court she has a cultural obligation to "protect lands that are being slaughtered", after she was charged with trespassing while protesting in a logging coupe.

item.title: State of Origin: Queensland Maroons' furious flair comes back to bite them
item.description: The Maroons under Billy Slater have gone all in on speed, attack and flair. On the biggest stage in the Origin decider, when things went against them, it all turned a bit too frantic.

item.title: Melbourne air force base records highest results among elevated PFAS levels in young sparrows
item.description: Blood samples found levels of the "forever chemicals" in sparrows near contaminated industrial and military sites were 10 times higher than those in a comparison population.

item.title: Narendra Modi receives red carpet welcome in Melbourne as fans and protesters gather
item.description: A large swathe of the Indian diaspora wants to warmly welcome Narendra Modi to Melbourne, but others demand Australia put pressure on him over his human rights record.

item.title: World Cup: Racist commentary called out in media coverage, hurting diversity at the top
item.description: Leading experts say racial stereotypes in World Cup coverage can be damaging for athletes' leadership aspirations and have an impact on Black communities more broadly.

item.title: Adelaide automotive dismantler penalised $25k for not giving adequate meal breaks
item.description: An automotive industry group made threats "to create fear" in a man who had brought legal action against his employer for not giving him adequate lunch breaks, a tribunal judgement has found.

item.title: Iran says it has struck US bases after Trump restarts attacks — as it happened
item.description: Tehran says it has struck US military infrastructure in the Middle East after Washington launched a fresh series of strikes against Iran to "degrade their ability to threaten freedom of navigation" in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Fears for jobs as SunRice cuts shifts due to drought and water buybacks
item.description: Communities in southern NSW fear large cutbacks to rice processing could put hundreds of people out of work and decimate regional economies.

item.title: NT Aboriginal language speakers call for stronger language revival efforts
item.description: Aboriginal language custodians release a road map for the survival and revival of Northern Territory Indigenous languages.

item.title: V/Line train services slowly resume in Victoria following Telstra outage
item.description: V/Line is gradually resuming services from midday after a Telstra outage brought the state's regional train network to a standstill.

item.title: Angus Taylor backs shadow minister for test-calling Triple Zero — as it happened
item.description: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor has backed Sarah Henderson's decision to test-call Triple Zero, saying she was "doing her job".

item.title: In clash of AFL titans Fremantle and Sydney, who will emerge as premiership favourites?
item.description: Fremantle take on Sydney at Perth Stadium tonight, in a match boasting storylines as compelling as early-era Melrose Place. Who will emerge from the clash as premiership favourites?

item.title: Live: Telstra confirms Triple Zero services are back online
item.description: Triple Zero services are back online following Telstra's outage. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

item.title: Why a Telstra outage has stopped some trains in regional New South Wales
item.description: Trains relying on the 4G network of freight rail track operator ARTC went down amid the Telstra failure.

item.title: Wimbledon 2026: British wildcard Arthur Fery sets up semifinal clash with Alexander Zverev
item.description: After growing up just five minutes from Wimbledon, Arthur Fery is now through to the semifinals at his home grand slam.

item.title: Union raised safety concerns with freight train operator day before Berrima derailment
item.description: The rail union says it raised safety concerns with a freight train operator about 24 hours before a major derailment in the New South Wales Southern Highlands.

item.title: Five First Nations musicians redefining classical music in Australia
item.description: From James Henry to Nardi Simpson, Aaron Wyatt, Adam Manning and Nina Korbe, here are First Nations voices making waves in Australia’s classical music scene.

item.title: Scammers use AI image to target parents of WA missing man
item.description: Scammers have sent the parents of a missing West Australian man an AI-generated image of their son and demanded thousands of dollars in exchange for his safety.

item.title: NSW coach Laurie Daley slams 'hidden agendas' after State of Origin triumph
item.description: Laurie Daley has the last laugh over critics who hounded him ahead of the State of Origin decider at Lang Park.

item.title: Noosa locals, businesses outraged by Doonella Lake Bridge traffic diversions
item.description: Noosa locals are worried that businesses won't survive six months' worth of traffic diversions while a crucial bridge is upgraded.

item.title: Researchers harvest water from the air to grow native seedlings and rehydrate the Wheatbelt
item.description: Can water be made from air in WA's Wheatbelt? Researchers are putting an invention to the test.

item.title: Telstra outage: 'Software defect solution' in place after hundreds unable to call Triple Zero in outage
item.description: Telstra has implemented a fresh "solution" to resolve a secondary software defect that left hundreds of customers unable to make Triple Zero calls during a nationwide network outage.

item.title: Ukraine's Kostyuk extends Wimbledon run and slams IOC decision on Russia
item.description: Ukraine's Marta Kostyuk is through to the Wimbledon semifinals. The 24-year-old says she finds it "tough" as her hometown of Kyiv continues to come under attack from Russia.

item.title: Footage showing ruins of Khamenei's compound released
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Albury families concerned over axing of local NDIS-funded program
item.description: An Albury-based NDIS provider says an upcoming change to NDIS funding is one of the reasons it will discontinue a program that builds skills and confidence in those living with a disability.

item.title: Queensland police check on 21 callers after national Telstra outage
item.description: Queensland police confirmed multiple emergency calls across the state failed to connect to Triple Zero during the nationwide Telstra outage.

item.title: Emmys 2026: Widow's Bay sneaks in, Rob Reiner honoured and more Emmy surprises
item.description: Plus Connor Storrie nabs his first nom but not for Heated Rivalry, Hacks soars on a swan song and more successes, surprises and snubs from today's Emmy nominations.

item.title: The Desert Champions Way: Outback Camel Trail, 2026, starts with camel races in Jundah
item.description: "A mate rang me up and said, 'Do you want to have a go?'" That's all it took to get Jake Callagan in the saddle for the Jundah Camel races. The novice camel jockey took to it well, with a swag of blue, red and green ribbons to take home.

item.title: WA gold project Warrawoona fined $118,000 over threatened bat
item.description: A Western Australian gold miner has been hit with a fine of more than $100,000 for failing to protect a vulnerable bat species.

item.title: Search for alternatives to paraquat continues after usage restricted
item.description: Australia buys most of its paraquat from China, but is investigating ways to become less reliant on the chemical.

item.title: US judge says E Jean Carroll can receive $8 million payment in Trump sexual abuse case
item.description: A Manhattan judge orders the payment to the former Elle magazine advice columnist, who a jury found was sexually abused by Donald Trump.

item.title: FIFA president Gianni Infantino referred to Olympics ethics investigators over Donald Trump's World Cup intervention
item.description: Gianni Infantino's close ties with Donald Trump continue to land him in hot water after the US President's World Cup intervention.

item.title: Trump says US is hitting Iran 'very hard'
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Democrats' Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner suspends midterm campaign after sexual assault accusation
item.description: The Democratic Senate candidate in the US state of Maine has suspended his midterm election campaign after a string of controversies, including an accusation of sexual assault.

item.title: Envoy to combat antisemitism calls for independent oversight committee to review ABC's compliance with charter
item.description: The ABC's editorial director Gavin Fang tells a royal commission it was a "bad mistake" an on-air correction about a statement on Gaza was not made on the same program the error first occurred.

item.title: Neil the seal 'may have moved on', Tasmanian wildlife experts say
item.description: Authorities in Tasmania say it appears Neil the seal may have left his resting spot at a Hobart beach and returned to sea.

item.title: US strikes Iran and reinstates oil sanctions, but Tehran has already raked in billions
item.description: The ceasefire between the United States and Iran is fraying and Washington has attempted to crush the regime's oil sales, but Tehran made its move when the blockade lifted weeks ago.

item.title: I'd always wanted to carry a baby. Cancer meant I couldn't
item.description: The grief of losing the chance to have baby is something I expect to carry forever. But I'm becoming more comfortable with the unconventional life I'm building with my partner April.

item.title: Country musician Jeanette Wormald experiences copyright dispute and AI website woes
item.description: Country-folk musician Jeanette Wormald was shocked when she found a fake AI-generated website "tribute" to her, in place of a site she thought had lapsed. But then she found disputes over her copyright on her music.

item.title: Gold Coast rental prices reach record-high in June, making it Australia's most expensive market
item.description: Real estate experts say the Gold Coast's high concentration of luxury housing and persistent shortages in new construction are sustaining upward pressure on rentals.

item.title: Our Watch condemns Australian boxer's joke about domestic violence spike on State of Origin night
item.description: A leading violence prevention organisation says an Australian boxer's joke about domestic violence is unacceptable.

item.title: Trainee surgeon Ryan Cho pleads guilty to secretly filming hundreds of victims
item.description: Ryan Cho has pleaded guilty to 13 charges, including stalking, producing an intimate image and installing an optical surveillance device.

item.title: Life returns to streets around damaged NYC high-rise
item.description: A Midtown Manhattan high-rise being converted into apartments has been stabilised after buckling support columns triggered evacuations and fears of a collapse.

item.title: How Hudson Young and Liam Martin bashed their way to State of Origin glory
item.description: Liam Martin and Hudson Young don't like to point fingers, unless it comes time to decide which of the New South Wales backrowers is madder.

item.title: Fremantle's quality talls and settled defence key as Dockers chase first-ever AFL flag
item.description: Fremantle has lost its 14-game winning streak with a loss to GWS, but what are the keys for the Dockers to keep going and set up a tilt at their first flag? Cody Atkinson and Sean Lawson investigate.

item.title: Egypt demands FIFA investigate referees after controversial World Cup loss to Argentina
item.description: Egypt remains incensed at French referee Francois Letexier after his performance in the Round of 16 clash against Argentina at the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Swimming Australia to take proactive, world-first approach to 'scary' fake news stories
item.description: In a world-first initiative, Swimming Australia is taking a proactive approach to deal with clickbait and fake news headlines about its athletes, including Olympic champion Mollie O'Callaghan, who has been targeted in the past.

item.title: Search continues after Boeing cargo plane crashes off coast of Pakistan
item.description: Rescue crews battle rough sea conditions to recover parts of a Boeing cargo plane that crashed into the Arabian Sea. Five crew members remain missing.

item.title: Video shared by US Central Command of strikes against Iran
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Telstra outage leaves elderly woman with dementia 'frightened', as senator's comments labelled 'irresponsible'
item.description: An SA woman says her elderly mother was left "frightened" during the Telstra outage after a fall. Meanwhile, SA police have said they have no record of any deaths during the network failure, despite a senator's claims.

item.title: Maryborough teenager allegedly told police he had planned mass murder for months
item.description: According to court documents, the 13-year-old told police he had planned mass murder for many months, had been inspired by other school shooters and massacres.

item.title: More than 1,300 staff receive $2 million in back pay from disability support provider Yooralla
item.description: Fair Work Ombudsman Anna Booth says Yooralla's underpayment of staff serves as a warning to employers to comply with minimum payment laws.

item.title: Sixteen-year-old boy charged with murder after stabbing in Taree
item.description: Police say a 16-year-old boy accused of murdering a 36-year-old man on the NSW Mid North Coast was known to the deceased.

item.title: Gout Gout ruled out of World Under-20 athletics titles after training injury
item.description: Australian athletics star Gout Gout is ruled out of this year's World Under-20 titles in Oregon after a training injury in Brisbane.

item.title: Landsdale MP Daniel Pastorelli gets plum WA cabinet spot after Paul Papalia resignation
item.description: New Landsdale MP Daniel Pastorelli, the former chief of staff to both Mark McGowan and Roger Cook, will be parachuted into cabinet to replace Paul Papalia, the ABC understands.

item.title: NT Police charge 34yo man with murder after 17yo girl found dead in Galiwin'ku home
item.description: A 34-year-old man has been charged over the alleged murder of his teenage partner, a 17-year-old girl who Northern Territory Police believe "died of blunt forced injuries" in a remote community.
